title: Enforce the order state graph
description: Allow only explicit transitions between order states and reject illegal jumps.
body: Use when an order moves through pending, paid, shipped, delivered, cancelled, and refunded. Do not let any code set an arbitrary status — unguarded writes produce impossible states like shipped-but-unpaid. Actions: define the allowed transition graph, guard every status change against it, and persist the transition with its trigger. Validation signals include every persisted transition being a legal edge and rejected illegal jumps. Risk boundary: a cancelled or refunded order must not transition back to fulfilment — treat backward money-affecting transitions as requiring human review.
